sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 25

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 25:1
items.html: (to the question) From the discernment of the Rucha Kadma'a [first spirit], the lower one and the upper one are regarded as brothers to one another, for from this discernment both emerged from one father, which is the upper-upper. Yet from there the lower one still receives no form until it comes to a second Zivug [coupling] in the place of the upper one, and then it receives the form of the embryo, as is known. Therefore, from the discernment of the building of the Partzuf, the lower one is regarded as a son to the upper one, for the upper one is the particular cause of the emanation of the lower Partzuf. (Item 8)
